---
title: Test Filename on Code Blocks
layout: single
---

## Code block with filename

```{.python filename="app.py"}
from shiny.express import input, ui
import shinyswatch

ui.input_slider("num", "Number:", min=10, max=100, value=30)
```

## Code block without filename

```{.python}
print("hello world")
```

## Another filename

```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
project:
  type: website
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
```

## Plain text with filename

```{filename="Makefile"}
build:
	echo "building..."
```

## Filename with code-line-numbers

```{.python filename="app.py" code-line-numbers="true"}
from shiny.express import input, ui

ui.input_slider("num", "Number:", min=10, max=100, value=30)
```

## Filename with custom class

```{.python filename="example.py" .custom-class}
print("hello world")
```

## Filename with shortcodes=false

```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml" shortcodes="false"}
project:
  type: website
```

## Filename with path

```{.python filename="src/app.py"}
from shiny.express import input, ui

ui.input_slider("num", "Number:", min=10, max=100, value=30)
```

## Long filename

```{.python filename="this-is-a-very-long-descriptive-filename-that-might-overflow.py"}
print("testing label overflow")
```

## R code with filename

```{.r filename="analysis.R"}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point()
```
